user_query: Show me a one-bowl dinner for Mother that's plant-based and light - something with grains and veggies all mixed together.

selected_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 6 > Mother > Day 5 > Dinner > Tofu Veggie Curry — Smoky Bake

node_data.name: Tofu Veggie Curry — Smoky Bake
node_data.short_description: Smoky baked tofu with mixed vegetables in curry sauce, served over rice.
node_data.meal_suggestion: Best as a quick weeknight meal. Typical serving: 1 cup cooked rice with a generous portion of tofu-vegetable curry. For more protein, add beans; to reduce carbs, swap rice for cauliflower rice. Leftovers keep 3-4 days in the fridge.
node_data.nutrition_note: Plant-based protein from tofu provides essential amino acids; fiber from vegetables and rice supports fullness. Sodium may be high if you use store-bought curry sauce—consider a low-sodium option or homemade curry to reduce salt. Baking tofu with minimal oil keeps fat modest while still adding flavor.

selected_nodes.reasoning: Tofu Veggie Curry — Smoky Bake is explicitly plant-based and served over rice, which matches a one-bowl style (grains + mixed vegetables + tofu). It is vegetable-forward and can be prepared light (suggestions note swapping rice for cauliflower rice or reducing sauce).
